name: grep
label: grep
description: Search file contents using ripgrep: rg --json -n -e '<regex>' -g '<glob>' <path(s)...>.
promptGuidelines: Use grep for fast content search when you know the text(s) or regex(es) to find.

regexes.description: Ripgrep-compatible regex pattern(s) to search for. Provide one or more regexes; each regex is passed as `-e <regex>` and multiple regexes use OR semantics. Searches are line-oriented; inline flags like `(?i)` can be used.

paths.description: Path(s) to search in. Provide one or more directories or files. If omitted, the current working directory is used.

globs.description: Glob filter(s) for files to search. Provide one or more globs; each glob is passed as `-g <glob>`; use `!` for exclusions. If omitted, no glob filters are applied.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return globally. If omitted, defaults to {{defaultLimit}}.

limitPerFile.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return per file. If omitted, defaults to {{defaultLimitPerFile}}.

maxCharsPerMatch.description: Maximum number of characters to show per matching line. If omitted, defaults to {{defaultMaxCharsPerMatch}}.

noIgnore.description: Include files ignored by .gitignore, .ignore, or other ripgrep ignore rules. If true, passes `--no-ignore`. If omitted, defaults to false.

hidden.description: Include hidden files. If true, passes `--hidden`. If omitted, defaults to false. Avoid using this argument unless intentionally searching hidden files; when using it, pair it with a targeted regex, glob, or path.
